The syntax for finding files modified more than 31 days ago would be something like find /path/to/directory -mtime +31 -type f. The -type f ensures we only check files. The -mtime +31 means files modified within the last 31 days. The -exec is used with a shell command, so the -exec part would execute the rm command. 

Putting that together, the bash command would be: find /testdata -mtime +31 -type f -exec rm -f {} \;
